created_components.runner_support: scripts/train_controlled_v6b_minimal.py optional Stage45-B family holdout flags

reports: eligible holdout families by min row count

runner_family_holdout_behavior.enabled_behavior: dev rows are records whose internal resolved family equals --stage45-holdout-family; train rows are all other records
failure_conditions: zero training rows after holdout split
runner_family_holdout_behavior.stage44b2_compatibility: Stage44-B2 selection may be used and will operate only on the current internal holdout dev split.
runner_family_holdout_behavior.stage43_external_eval: remains optional and is never implicitly enabled by Stage45-B

guardrails: Do not read Stage43 external reports to set thresholds or families.
Do not use external examples, metrics, labels, or predictions.
allowed_claims: Stage45-B creates an internal family manifest scaffold.
Stage45-B creates optional internal leave-family-out split support.
Stage45-B is designed to diagnose controlled-family robustness before Stage45-C redesign.
Default runner behavior is unchanged when Stage45 flags are off.
disallowed_claims: Do not claim VitaminC transfer success.
Do not claim naturalistic fact-verification generalization.
Do not claim Stage45-B improves performance.
Do not use Stage43-B1 for tuning, calibration, thresholds, checkpoint selection, loss selection, data generation, or model selection.
remaining_risks: If controlled data lacks explicit family metadata, all rows may fall back to unknown_family.
Tiny families may be unsuitable as holdouts without aggregation or more internal data.
Leave-family-out robustness is an internal proxy, not external validation.
next_stage: Stage45-C: evidence sufficiency contrast curriculum or auxiliary diagnostic, selected only by internal leave-family-out criteria
